introduction: Qutbul Madar, also known as Hazrat Syed Badiuddin Zinda Shah Madar, is a revered figure in the Sufi tradition. He was originally from Syria, born in the city of Halab (now known as Aleppo) in 856 CE/AD. He later migrated to India, where he founded the Madariyya Sufi brotherhood. The official website of Qutbul Madar provides a wealth of information about his life, teachings, and the Sufi brotherhood he established.
key-features: Here are some key features you can find on the website

sections.title: Urs of Sarkaar Syed Badiuddin Zinda Shah Madar
sections.description: The website provides information about the Urs (death anniversary) of Hazrat Syed Badiuddin Zinda Shah Madar, which is observed on the 13th, 14th, and 15th of February.

sections.title: Dargah Hazrat Syed Badiuddin Zinda Shah Madar
sections.description: The website offers details about the Dargah (shrine) of Hazrat Syed Badiuddin Zinda Shah Madar.

sections.title: Jamia Arbiya Madarul Uloom Madinatul Auliya
sections.description: This is an educational institution where needy students receive education. The website encourages visitors to support these students through donations.

sections.title: Mazar Shareef Chadar Poshi
sections.description: The website provides information about the monthly rituals of Chadar Poshi (offering of a ceremonial cloth) and Phool Poshi (offering of flowers) at the Dargah.

sections.title: Contact Information
sections.description: The website provides contact information for those who wish to participate in these rituals or support the educational institution.

sections.title: Useful Books
sections.description: The website offers useful books in English, Urdu, Hindi, and Farsi.

articles.title: UNESCO recently enlists Chamba - The Heritage City
articles.description: A Syrian Sufi, Sayyid Badiuddin Ahmad Zinda Shah Madar, played a foundational role in the spiritual and cultural evolution of Chamba. Born in Aleppo (Halab), Syria in the 9th century, Shah Madar was a Hasani-Hussaini Sayyid endowed with extraordinary spiritual qualities from birth. After extensive spiritual training under renowned saints like Bayazeed Bustami, he was commanded to spread Sufism, particularly in India where social evils prevailed. Arriving in India in 859 AD, he travelled extensively across regions including Gujarat, Rajasthan, Uttar Pradesh, Bengal, Kashmir, and Nepal. During his visit to Chamba, Shah Madar performed miraculous acts that protected the region from natural calamities and evil forces, paving the way for peaceful settlement. His blessings influenced Raja Sahil Verman to establish Chamba as the capital. The Madari Sufi tradition deeply shaped Chamba’s socio-cultural heritage, supported later by Mughal rulers. Today, Shah Madar’s Chillah in Chamba stands as a spiritual and historical landmark, with potential to be developed as a heritage tourism site.

articles.title: A Pleasant Sight of Old Khanqahi Traditions Accomplished at Makanpur
articles.description: Makanpur Sharif, sanctified by the presence of Zinda Shah Madar in 818 AH, remains a living center of Madari Sufi traditions. For centuries, this Khanqah has preserved the authentic customs of Sajjadagi, especially during the annual Urs held in Jumada Awwal. A unique tradition observed on the 16th and 17th Jumada Awwal involves the hereditary Sajjada Nashin enthroning on Takht-e-Sajjadagi while a sacred boat containing the Holy Quran is ceremoniously presented. This ritual symbolizes the Prophet Muhammad’s teachings comparing his Ahl-e-Bait to the Ark of Prophet Nuh and emphasizing adherence to the Quran and his family. Despite challenges posed by false claimants, the hereditary lineage of the Madari Sajjada Nashins has remained intact. The Khanqah continues to serve as a spiritual center where faith is renewed, disciples are initiated, and communal langar is distributed, preserving a 600-year-old legacy.

articles.title: Biographical Sketch of Sayyid Badiuddin Ahmad Zinda Shah Madar
articles.description: Sayyid Badiuddin Ahmad Zinda Shah Madar, also known as Qutb-ul-Madar and Madar-e-Alam, was born on 1st Shawwal 242 AH (856 CE) in Aleppo, Syria. A direct descendant of the Prophet Muhammad (SAWS) through both Imam Hasan and Imam Hussain, he displayed miraculous signs from birth. By fourteen, he mastered both exoteric and esoteric sciences. He received Khilafat from Sultan-ul-Arifeen Bayazeed Bistami and later direct spiritual training from the Prophet Muhammad (SAWS) and Mawla Ali. Commanded to spread Islam in India, he arrived in 281 AH after a miraculous sea journey. For over five centuries, he travelled across India propagating Islam, establishing spiritual centers, and guiding millions. He founded and strengthened several Sufi orders including Madariyya and Owaisiyya. He passed away in 1417 AD in Makanpur, where his Dargah remains a major center of devotion and spiritual learning.

introduction.p1: Qutbul Madar is such a shining sun in the realm of Wilayat that his spiritual light illuminated the hearts, minds and souls of Muslims across Asia and Europe. The rays of this radiant sun enlightened not only the Indian subcontinent but also the Khanqahs and Madrasas of Europe and Africa.
introduction.p2: It is extremely difficult to encompass the traits of a saintly life that spanned five hundred and ninety-six years. This brief account is presented as a humble attempt to include his blessed biography among the records of history.

identity.title: Name & Titles
identity.titles: Shah Madar, Zinda Shah Madar, Shaikh Madar, Qutbul Madar, Madarul Alameen, Zinda Pir, Zinda Vali, Madar-e-Azam, Sar Giroh-e-Ahle Tabaqat, Pir Zinda Madar
identity.amongSufis: Among the Sufis he is remembered as Zindan-e-Suf and Abdullah.

birth.title: Birth
birth.content: He was born on 1st Shawwal 242 A.H. in Halab (Syria) on Monday, the day of Eid. His blessed birth illuminated the house of his father Sayyed Ali Halabi.

dreamsBeforeBirth.title: Glad Tidings Before Birth
dreamsBeforeBirth.content: His mother Bibi Fatima Sania saw a radiant sun rising in her courtyard whose light spread far and wide. His father saw Prophet Muhammad ﷺ in a dream giving glad tidings of a noble son named Badiuddin who would become a great saint of his era.

miraclesAtBirth.title: Miracles at Birth
miraclesAtBirth.content: At the moment of birth, voices of 'Allah' were heard from all directions. The newborn immediately went into Sajda and recited the Kalima in eloquent language.

genealogy.title: Sacred Genealogy
genealogy.fatherSide: From his father's side he traces lineage to Imam Husain (RA) through Imam Zainul Abideen, Imam Baqar and Imam Jafar Sadiq.
genealogy.motherSide: From his mother's side he traces lineage to Imam Hasan (RA) through Hasan Musna and other noble ancestors.

education.title: Early Education
education.content: At the age of four years, four months and four days, he began his education. By fourteen, he had mastered Quran, Hadith, jurisprudence and religious sciences. Prophet Khizr adorned him with Ilm-e-Laduni and rare spiritual sciences.

spiritualTraining.title: Spiritual Training & Khilafat
spiritualTraining.content: He received Khilafat in the Jafaria Husainia Silsila and trained under Sultanul Arefin Bayazid Bustami. Under his guidance he completed stages of mysticism.

meetingProphet.title: Spiritual Presence at Madina
meetingProphet.content: After Hajj, he was spiritually honored in the presence of Prophet Muhammad ﷺ who instructed him to spread Islam in Hindustan and illuminate hearts with the light of Tawheed.

missionToIndia.title: Mission to Hindustan
missionToIndia.content: With firm faith, he set out toward India. He fasted constantly and relied entirely upon divine sustenance.

journeyToIndia.title: Journey by Sea
journeyToIndia.content: While preaching on a ship to India, a storm destroyed the vessel. By Allah’s will he survived on a plank and reached Gujarat.

arrivalKhambat.title: Arrival at Khambat (282 A.H.)
arrivalKhambat.content: He reached Khambat in Gujarat where he began spreading Islam. Prophet Khizr guided him spiritually at that sacred place.

maqam.title: Maqam-e-Samadiyat
maqam.content: He attained Maqam-e-Samadiyat. His face radiated divine نور such that whoever saw him remembered Allah and fell into Sajda. He veiled his face to protect the devotees from the overwhelming spiritual light.

madarShareefChadarPoshi:
“Madar e Shareef – Chadar Poshi” is a sacred tradition observed at the Qutbul Madar Dargah in Makanpur, where devotees have the opportunity to offer a symbolic cloth, known as a “chadar,” at the revered tomb of the saint. This act of devotion holds profound significance for believers, symbolizing reverence, gratitude, and a deep spiritual connection with the saint and the divine.

The tradition of Chadar Poshi is deeply rooted in the veneration of saints and holy figures, who are believed to possess divine grace and blessings. As devotees approach the tomb of the saint, they carry with them a chadar, often adorned with intricate embroidery, precious fabrics, or heartfelt messages expressing their devotion and prayers.

The act of placing the chadar on the saint’s tomb is a gesture of offering and surrender, symbolizing the devotee’s willingness to lay their hopes, fears, and aspirations at the feet of the saint. It is believed that the chadar absorbs the blessings and spiritual energy of the saint, serving as a conduit for divine grace to flow into the life of the devotee.

Madar e Shareef – Chadar Poshi is not merely a ritualistic practice; it is a profound expression of faith and devotion that transcends the material realm. For believers, the act of offering a chadar is imbued with spiritual significance, symbolizing a sacred bond between the devotee and the saint, and an acknowledgment of the saint’s intercession on their behalf.

The chadar itself becomes a tangible symbol of the devotee’s connection with the saint, serving as a reminder of the blessings received and the prayers offered. Many devotees cherish the chadar as a sacred keepsake, treasuring it as a token of their spiritual journey and the divine grace they have experienced.

Participating in Madar e Shareef – Chadar Poshi is a deeply personal and meaningful experience for devotees, offering them a moment of communion with the divine and a sense of solace and reassurance in their spiritual quest. It is a tradition that transcends barriers of language, culture, and background, uniting believers in a shared reverence for the saint and the transformative power of faith.

In essence, Madar e Shareef – Chadar Poshi is a sacred tradition that embodies the timeless principles of devotion, humility, and surrender. It is a testament to the enduring legacy of the saint and the profound impact of their teachings on the lives of countless devotees who continue to seek solace and guidance at their holy shrine.

langar.title: Langar – Bhandara
langar.subtitle: Nourishing the Body, Nurturing the Soul
langar.content:
"Langar – Bhandara" is a compassionate and altruistic service provided by the organization at the Qutbul Madar Dargah in Makanpur. Rooted in the ethos of selflessness and service to humanity, this initiative aims to alleviate hunger and provide nourishment to those in need, regardless of their socio-economic status.

The essence of Langar – Bhandara lies in the tradition of communal dining, where individuals from all walks of life come together to share a meal as equals. At the heart of this service is the belief that food not only nourishes the body but also nurtures the soul, fostering a sense of unity, compassion, and goodwill among all who partake.

Every day, volunteers and devotees of the dargah come together to prepare and serve wholesome meals to the less fortunate members of the community. The menu typically consists of simple yet nutritious dishes, such as rice, lentils, vegetables, chapatis, and sometimes sweet treats, all prepared with care and devotion.

The Langar – Bhandara service is open to all, regardless of caste, creed, or religion, embodying the spirit of inclusivity and equality espoused by the teachings of the dargah. Whether someone is a pilgrim seeking blessings, a traveler passing through, or a local resident facing hardship, they are welcomed with open arms and a warm meal.

Participating in Langar – Bhandara is not just about receiving sustenance; it is also an opportunity for individuals to experience the joy of giving and serving others. Volunteers play a crucial role in this service, dedicating their time and effort to ensure that no one goes hungry and that everyone feels valued and cared for.

Beyond meeting the immediate need for food, Langar – Bhandara serves as a beacon of hope and compassion in the community. It provides a safe haven for those facing food insecurity, offering them a moment of respite from the struggles of daily life and reminding them that they are not alone in their journey.

The impact of Langar – Bhandara extends far beyond the confines of the dining area, touching the lives of all who encounter it with its message of kindness and generosity. It fosters a sense of solidarity and unity within the community, bridging divides and fostering a culture of empathy and support for one another.

dargah-mannat.title: Dargah Mannat
dargah-mannat.subtitle: Entrust Your Prayers to the Divine
dargah-mannat.content:
"Dargah Mannat" is a unique service offered at the Qutbul Madar Dargah located in Makanpur. This sacred site holds a significant place in the hearts of believers who seek the fulfillment of their prayers, wishes, and desires. The essence of "Mannat," or prayer, lies at the core of this service, promising devotees a ray of hope in their moments of need.

At Dargah Mannat, individuals entrust their prayers and wishes to the divine power believed to reside within the sacred premises. The process begins with the devotee expressing their heartfelt desires, whether it's for health, prosperity, success, or any other aspect of life they seek divine intervention for. These prayers are offered with utmost sincerity and devotion, accompanied by rituals and ceremonies that have been practiced for generations.

One of the distinguishing features of Dargah Mannat is the belief among devotees that prayers made at this holy site have a higher likelihood of being answered. It is believed that the spiritual energy and blessings of the saints and holy figures associated with the dargah amplify the potency of these prayers, making them more likely to be fulfilled.

The process of seeking Mannat involves not only the act of prayer but also a deep sense of faith and surrender to the divine will. Devotees come with faith-filled hearts, trusting in the power of the dargah to intercede on their behalf and bring about the desired outcome.

Visitors to Dargah Mannat often come from far and wide, seeking solace, guidance, and blessings for various aspects of their lives. Whether facing challenges, seeking blessings for new beginnings, or expressing gratitude for blessings received, devotees find a sense of peace and reassurance in the act of prayer at this revered site.

The atmosphere at Dargah Mannat is one of reverence and devotion, with devotees offering their prayers amidst the soothing chants of hymns and the fragrance of incense wafting through the air. It is a place where the boundaries between the material and the spiritual blur, and believers find themselves immersed in a profound sense of connection with the divine.

In the hearts of those who visit Dargah Mannat, there exists a profound belief in the power of prayer and the divine grace that permeates the sacred space. It is a testament to the enduring faith of countless individuals who seek solace and divine intervention in the journey of life.

special-dua.title: Special Dua
special-dua.subtitle: Dedicated Prayers for Your Loved Ones
special-dua.content:
"Special Dua" is a bespoke service offered at the Qutbul Madar Dargah in Makanpur, where devotees have the opportunity to request dedicated prayers, or dua, for specific individuals or intentions close to their hearts. This personalized form of prayer is tailored to address the unique needs, challenges, or aspirations of the individual for whom the dua is being offered.

The essence of Special Dua lies in the power of focused and heartfelt prayer, believed to have the potential to invoke divine blessings and intervention in the lives of those being prayed for. Devotees approach the dargah with their intentions, whether it's for healing, protection, guidance, or any other aspect of life they seek divine assistance with.

The process of requesting a Special Dua typically involves communicating the specific intention or need to the custodians or spiritual leaders of the dargah, who then undertake the responsibility of offering dedicated prayers on behalf of the individual or individuals in question.

The prayers offered during Special Dua are characterized by their depth of sincerity, faith, and devotion. The custodians and spiritual leaders of the dargah invoke the divine through sacred rituals, recitations from holy texts, and heartfelt supplications, seeking divine mercy and blessings for the intended recipients of the dua.

What sets Special Dua apart is the personalized nature of the prayers, tailored to address the specific circumstances and challenges faced by the individuals for whom the dua is being offered. Whether it's for the health and well-being of a loved one, success in endeavors, resolution of conflicts, or any other pressing need, devotees find solace in knowing that their concerns are being earnestly and lovingly lifted up in prayer.

Participating in Special Dua is a deeply meaningful and spiritually enriching experience for both the individuals requesting the prayers and those offering them. It fosters a sense of community, compassion, and solidarity among believers, as they come together in the shared pursuit of divine grace and blessings.
